steps.title: Test Point P in inequality I
steps.content:
For inequality I: $y < -3x + 7$, substitute $x = 3$ and $y = -5$:
$$-5 < -3(3) + 7$$
$$-5 < -9 + 7$$
$$-5 < -2$$
This is true — point P satisfies inequality I.

steps.title: Test Point P in inequality II
steps.content:
For inequality II: $y > x - 5$, substitute $x = 3$ and $y = -5$:
$$-5 > 3 - 5$$
$$-5 > -2$$
This is false — point P does not satisfy inequality II.

Since P satisfies only inequality I, that matches choice A — A is correct.
